cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the 1974 World Bank study and what it concluded regarding economic growth in some backward countries. The options are A to D. First, I need to go back to the passage and find the relevant part. The passage mentions that a major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that "it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, wait, that wording is a bit tricky. Let me read it again. The exact sentence is: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, the study says that decades of growth have been of little benefit to a third of the population. That means two thirds of the population may have benefited? Or is it that only a third benefited?
